domain: intertidal zonation (rocky shore vertical stratification of organisms by tidal-exposure tolerance: spray-zone lichens, upper periwinkles, mid barnacles, lower mussels, subtidal kelp; each zone has specific desiccation/thermal/wave-energy stress profile; species evolve zone-specific tolerance via heat-shock proteins, gill-air storage)
specific_mechanism: Vertical stress gradient on rocky shore: tidal exposure ranges from 100% air (supralittoral) to 100% water (subtidal). Species occupy zones where tolerance × competition allow. Upper-zone species evolve high-tolerance traits (HSPs, desiccation resistance); lower-zone species evolve competitor-resistance. The stress gradient PRODUCES the community zonation as emergent specialization.
llm_application: Stress-stratified layer-wise specialization training: during pretraining, inject layer-dependent INPUT PERTURBATION (token swap probability, embedding noise σ) following a MONOTONICALLY DECREASING schedule with depth. Layer 1 sees 30% token swap + σ=0.2 noise; deeper layers see less; layer L sees zero perturbation. Forces early layers to learn EXPOSURE-TOLERANT robust representations (analogous to upper-zone HSP-bearing limpets) and deep layers to operate on stabilized signal (analogous to subtidal). Predicted: model robust to adversarial input perturbation by upper-layer absorption while maintaining clean deep reasoning. Distinguished from standard regularization which is depth-uniform.
novelty_claim: Layer-wise scaling work (Crown-Frame-Reverse 2509.06518), sparsity (2502.14770) modulate CAPACITY/SPARSITY per layer. Standard regularization (dropout, augmentation) is depth-uniform. The intertidal stress-gradient training (monotone decreasing layer-noise schedule for emergent tolerance-stratification) is not retrieved in surveyed prior art as a single discipline.
